subject: English
title: The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom

questions.text: How did the kind old couple treat their dog, Muko?
options: As if it were their own child
questions.explanation: The childless couple loved Muko dearly and fed him tidbits from their own chopsticks.

questions.text: How did Muko help the old man find the gold?
options: He dug at a spot under a pine tree
He brought a bag of coins
He led him to a cave
questions.explanation: Muko scratched the ground under a pine tree, leading the old man to discover a pile of gold.

questions.text: What did the greedy neighbors find when they forced the dog to dig?
questions.explanation: Instead of gold, the greedy couple found a foul-smelling dead kitten, which made them kill the dog in anger.

questions.text: The spirit of the dog appeared to the old man in a dream and told him to:
options: Cut down the pine tree and make a mortar
questions.explanation: The dog's spirit guided the man to create a mortar and mill from the pine tree over his grave.

questions.text: What happened when the kind couple used the mortar and mill?
options: Rice turned into gold and sauce into coins
questions.explanation: The magic tools turned their food into treasure, rewarding their kindness.

questions.text: What happened when the greedy neighbors borrowed the mortar and mill?
options: The food turned into a mass of worms
They were rewarded by the King
questions.explanation: Because of their wicked intentions, the tools produced only worms, leading them to chop the tools into firewood.

questions.text: What was the power of the ashes of the burnt mill?
options: They could make withered trees bloom
questions.explanation: The dog's spirit told the man that the ashes could bring life back to dead trees.

questions.text: The old man made a withered ________ tree bloom in front of the Daimyo.
questions.explanation: The old man sprinkled ashes on a dead cherry tree, making it blossom instantly.

questions.text: What happened to the greedy neighbor when he tried to imitate the kind old man?
options: He was punished and later died
The tree bloomed for him too
questions.explanation: The ashes only caused the Daimyo to sneeze, leading his guards to beat the greedy neighbor.

questions.text: This story is a famous folk tale from:
questions.explanation: 'The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom' is a traditional Japanese tale emphasizing that kindness is rewarded.
